Title: Target of rapamycin signaling in pea embryos is dependent on glutamine but detached from seed storage protein biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed nutrient reserve for germination and human/animal dietary protein
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Active TOR signaling delays or inhibits seed storage protein accumulation during early seed filling; TOR inhibition increases protein, starch, sucrose accumulation and embryo growth in young embryos.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TOR chemical inhibition with AZD5088/AZD; phosphoproteomics; RNA-seq; qPCR of storage protein genes; embryo metabolite profiling; embryo weight and protein quantification; ABA treatment interaction assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TOR activity in pea embryos is strongly dependent on exogenous glutamine and declines developmentally at the onset of seed filling. 2. Inhibition of TOR by AZD in 90–130 mg embryos increases protein accumulation, fresh/dry weight gain, sucrose and starch content, and storage protein transcript abundance. 3. TOR inhibition activates ABA-related transcriptional responses and makes embryos responsive to ABA-stimulated protein accumulation.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: TOR activity high in younger embryos up to ~150 mg FW, then drops sharply during seed filling.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High TOR activity in early developing cotyledons; low to undetectable in older filling embryos.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Phosphoproteomics in pea embryos; RNA-seq (NCBI SRA: PRJNA1251708)
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: RAPTOR1; S6K; RPS6; LARP1a; ATG13A; EIN2
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous glutamine, sucrose, AZD5088/AZD, ABA, auxinole, KCN, developmental stage, light/dark
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Despite canonical TOR outputs on translation/autophagy, storage protein synthesis in pea embryos is uncoupled from TOR promotion and instead associated with ABA signaling during maturation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Stage-specific reduction of TOR activity could potentially accelerate onset of storage reserve accumulation in legumes, but pleiotropic developmental effects and embryo-stage dependence require caution.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: TOR supports mitotic growth in young embryos; excessive inhibition may impair early embryogenesis or cause developmental defects, even if short-term reserve accumulation increases during early seed fill.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Identified as a TOR-dependent phosphoprotein in developing pea embryos, supporting TOR complex involvement in controlling storage protein accumulation state.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phosphoproteomics under glutamine versus AZD and glutamine+AZD treatments
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Regulatory-associated protein of TOR; TOR complex scaffold component with HEAT/raptor domains
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RAPTOR1 phosphosite abundance changes with TOR-active versus TOR-inhibited conditions. 2. The result supports operation of a canonical TOR complex in pea embryos during glutamine signaling.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Detected as downstream phosphoproteomic evidence rather than functionally manipulated focal gene.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: developing embryo vacuolar/amino acid storage compartment
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: organic nitrogen transport/storage supporting embryo metabolism
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TOR-dependent phosphorylation of vacuolar amino acid transporter YPQ1 suggests TOR regulates embryo amino acid compartmentation during glutamine signaling.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phosphoproteomics under glutamine versus AZD-containing treatments
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Two YPQ1 phosphosites increased in TOR-active glutamine-treated embryos. 2. YPQ1 is a novel putative downstream target linking TOR signaling to amino acid transport/homeostasis in seeds.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Novel putative TOR target; transporter role rather than enzyme or TF.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate amino acid partitioning in developing seeds, but direct functional evidence is lacking.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Unknown effects on seed amino acid homeostasis and vacuolar storage balance.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Protein kinase acting as TOR downstream effector
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Ribosomal protein S6 kinase, AGC kinase family
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: amino acid homeostasis and protein synthesis for growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: S6K Thr449 phosphorylation reports glutamine-induced TOR activation in mature Arabidopsis leaves, strongest in darkness and transient over 1–4 h.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: S6K1-HA immunoblotting of phospho-Thr449 after amino acid, sucrose, light/dark and AZD treatments
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: translation/protein synthesis signaling and amino acid metabolic state
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: RPS6 and translation-associated processes downstream of TOR-S6K axis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key TOR readout demonstrating glutamine-responsive nutrient signaling in leaves.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Glutamine; light; sucrose; TOR inhibition by AZD
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts in TOR signaling cascade downstream of TORC1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among amino acids tested, glutamine most strongly stimulates S6K Thr449 phosphorylation in mature Arabidopsis leaves. 2. The response is transient and predominantly observed in darkness. 3. AZD abolishes phosphorylation, confirming TOR dependence.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Assayed in mature leaves; higher utility in overexpression line than endogenous signal.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Darkness versus light; glutamine; sucrose; AZD5088
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for S6K1-HA reporter line
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a signaling reporter rather than nutrient-metabolism causal gene in seed storage protein phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited direct use; suitable as a marker for TOR activation status.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Ribosomal protein and TOR pathway phospho-readout
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RPS6 Ser240 phosphorylation marks glutamine-dependent TOR activation in pea embryos and declines during development as embryos enter seed filling.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Immunoblotting of phospho-Ser240 during embryo development and nutrient/inhibitor treatments; phosphoproteomics
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: translation signaling/state marker associated with reserve accumulation transition
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key in vivo molecular readout linking glutamine supply to TOR activity in developing embryos.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Glutamine; auxin-dependent signaling; respiration; AZD inhibition; developmental stage
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RPS6-Ser240 phosphorylation in pea embryos depends specifically on glutamine, not sucrose, glucose, ammonium nitrate, or other amino acids tested. 2. Phosphorylation is blocked by AZD, auxinole, and cyanide. 3. Developmental decline of RPS6 phosphorylation parallels reduced TOR activity at the onset of seed filling.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Phosphorylation high in young embryos before seed filling; low in older embryos.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing cotyledons, strongest in 90–150 mg embryos.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Glutamine dose; light/dark; auxinole; KCN; AZD5088; developmental stage
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as the principal biochemical readout of embryo TOR activity, not directly manipulated.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful biomarker for embryo TOR activity during reserve deposition studies.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed reserve proteins for maturation and germination
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ABI5 transcript abundance increases upon TOR inhibition, consistent with activation of ABA signaling that promotes storage protein gene expression in pea embryos.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; normalized transcript counts after AZD treatment; ABA interaction assays with protein accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation within ABA signaling network
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: seed maturation and storage protein biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes seed storage protein genes and other ABA-responsive maturation genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Seed storage protein genes; LEA genes; raffinose family oligosaccharide biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Represents a core ABA signaling node whose induction under TOR inhibition is associated with enhanced storage reserve program.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABI5 is upregulated in pea embryos after TOR inhibition. 2. AZD-treated embryos show enhanced ABA-responsive reserve accumulation, and ABA further stimulates protein accumulation only when TOR is inhibited. 3. The data support TOR–ABA antagonism controlling the onset of storage protein synthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by AZD in 115–130 mg embryos after 4 h.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Early seed-filling embryos under TOR inhibition.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: AZD5088; exogenous ABA; early seed fill stage
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a transcriptional responder in the TOR–ABA module rather than directly genetically manipulated.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential leverage point for improving legume seed storage reserve deposition through ABA sensitivity tuning.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced ABA signaling may affect desiccation responses, dormancy, or growth timing.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SnRK2.6 transcript is induced by TOR inhibition, supporting activation of ABA signaling associated with storage protein synthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein phosphorylation signaling within ABA pathway
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ABA-responsive maturation program linked to storage reserve deposition
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Supports ABA-responsive transcription including ABI5-associated outputs
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Marker and probable mediator of ABA pathway activation when TOR is inhibited during early seed filling.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of reciprocal ABA–TOR regulation discussed in paper
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SnRK2.6 transcript abundance rises after AZD treatment. 2. This supports activation of ABA signaling as TOR activity falls. 3. The result is consistent with enhanced reserve accumulation under TOR inhibition.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by AZD in early filling embryos.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Early seed fill under TOR inhibition.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be useful for tuning ABA responsiveness during seed maturation.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible effects on dormancy, stress responses, and developmental timing.
